questions.title: Two Sum Indexes
questions.prompt: Return the indexes of two different numbers that add up to a target. Return an empty list if no pair exists.
constraints: Each index can be used once.
hints: Store values you have seen with their index.
For each number, compute target minus number.
Check whether the complement was seen earlier.

questions.title: Balanced Brackets
questions.prompt: Return true if brackets (), [], and {} are correctly balanced and nested.
hints: Use a stack for opening brackets.
When you see a closing bracket, compare it with the stack top.
At the end, the stack should be empty.

questions.title: Longest Unique Window
questions.prompt: Return the length of the longest substring with no repeated characters.
constraints: Input length can be up to 10000.
hints: Keep a window with left and right indexes.
Remember the most recent index of each character.
Move the left side past duplicates.

questions.title: Group Anagrams
questions.prompt: Group words that are anagrams of each other. Return groups in any order.
hints: Anagrams share the same sorted letters.
Use the sorted word as a key.
Append each word to its key's group.

questions.title: Merge Sorted Lists
questions.prompt: Given two integer lists that are already sorted in ascending order, return one new sorted list containing every value from both lists.
hints: Use one pointer for each list.
Append the smaller current value.
Append any leftovers after one list ends.

questions.title: Course Prerequisite Chain
questions.prompt: Given pairs [course, prerequisite], return true if a target course eventually depends on a given prerequisite.
constraints: Pairs form a directed dependency graph.
hints: Create a lookup where each course points to the courses it needs first.
Start from the target course and follow its prerequisites one step at a time.
Return true as soon as the prerequisite you are looking for appears.

questions.title: Top K Frequent
questions.prompt: Return the k most frequent values in a list. If frequencies tie, any order is acceptable.
hints: Count each value first.
Sort or bucket by frequency.
Return the first k values.

questions.title: Matrix Row Sums
questions.prompt: Return a list containing the sum of each row in a matrix.
hints: Loop through each row.
Sum values in that row.
Append the row sum to the result.

questions.title: Rotate List Right
questions.prompt: Given a list and a number k, move each item k places to the right and wrap items from the end back to the front.
constraints: k may be larger than the list length.
hints: Use k modulo length.
Split the list at length - k.
Put the ending slice before the starting slice.

questions.title: First Missing Positive Small
questions.prompt: Return the smallest positive integer that does not appear in the list.
constraints: A set-based solution is fine for this version.
hints: Put all values in a set.
Start checking from 1.
Return the first positive number not in the set.

questions.title: Compress Runs
questions.prompt: Compress repeated adjacent characters as character plus count. Single characters still include count 1.
hints: Track the current character and count.
When the character changes, append the completed run.
Do not forget the final run.

questions.title: Valid Study Schedule
questions.prompt: Given meeting intervals [start, end], return false if any intervals overlap, otherwise true.
constraints: End time equal to next start time is allowed.
hints: Sort intervals by start time.
Compare each start with the previous end.
If start is less than previous end, there is overlap.

questions.title: Binary Search Insert Position
questions.prompt: Given a sorted list and target, return the index where the target is found or should be inserted.
hints: Use left and right bounds.
Move toward the first value greater than or equal to target.
When the loop ends, left is the insertion index.

questions.title: Count Islands
questions.prompt: Given a grid of 1s and 0s, count connected groups of 1s using up, down, left, and right neighbors.
hints: Scan the grid one cell at a time looking for a 1 that has not been handled yet.
When you find one, explore its up, down, left, and right neighbors to find the whole island.
Mark every cell in that island before you continue scanning, then add 1 to the island count.

questions.title: Min Stack Operations
questions.prompt: Process stack commands push x, pop, top, and min. Return outputs for top and min commands.
constraints: Commands are valid for this version.
hints: Use a normal stack for values.
Use a second stack for current minimums.
Update the minimum stack on push and pop.

questions.title: Normalize Email List
questions.prompt: Lowercase and trim each email, remove duplicates, and return the cleaned list in first-seen order.
constraints: Emails are strings; do not validate domain.
hints: Trim whitespace and lowercase first.
Use a set for cleaned emails already used.
Append only first occurrences.

questions.title: Prefix Search
questions.prompt: Return all words that start with a given prefix, preserving input order.
hints: Loop over words.
Use a starts-with check.
Collect matches in order.

questions.title: Window Average
questions.prompt: Return averages for every contiguous window of size k in a list of numbers.
hints: Compute the first window sum.
Slide by subtracting the outgoing value and adding the incoming value.
Divide each window sum by k.

questions.title: Nested List Depth Sum
questions.prompt: Given nested integers, return the sum of each integer multiplied by its depth. Top-level depth is 1.
constraints: Input is a nested list of integers.
hints: Write a helper with value and depth.
If the value is an integer, return value times depth.
If it is a list, recurse into each item with depth + 1.

questions.title: Most Recent Unique
questions.prompt: Given event names in chronological order, return the most recent event that appears exactly once. Return null if none exist.
hints: Count event frequencies.
Scan from the end of the list.
Return the first event whose count is one.

questions.title: Pair Sum Sorted
questions.prompt: Given a sorted list of integers, return true if two different values add up to the target.
constraints: The input list is sorted ascending.
hints: Because the list is sorted, the smallest value is on the left and the largest is on the right.
Add those two values and compare the sum to the target.
If the sum is too small, move left forward; if it is too large, move right backward.

questions.title: Binary Search Exact
questions.prompt: Given a sorted list and a target, return the target's index or -1 if it is not present.
hints: Keep two bounds: left starts at the first index and right starts at the last index.
Check the middle index between left and right.
If the middle value is not the target, move the bound that lets you ignore the impossible half.

questions.title: Help Desk Queue
questions.prompt: Process help desk commands. 'join name' adds a student to the back. 'serve' removes the front student and records their name. If the queue is empty, record 'none'.
hints: A queue means first student in, first student served.
For a join command, put the name at the back of the waiting list.
For a serve command, record the front name; if nobody is waiting, record 'none'.

questions.title: Recursive Digit Sum
questions.prompt: Return the sum of the digits of a non-negative integer using a recursive idea.
hints: A recursive solution needs a stopping case first: a one-digit number returns itself.
Use n % 10 to get the last digit.
Add that digit to the answer for the smaller number n // 10.

questions.title: Range Sum Queries
questions.prompt: Given a list of numbers and queries [left, right], return the sum inside each inclusive range.
hints: Make a running-total list where prefix[i] stores the sum before index i.
For a query from left to right, subtract the total before left from the total after right.
That formula is prefix[right + 1] - prefix[left].

questions.title: Merge Overlapping Intervals
questions.prompt: Given intervals [start, end], merge overlapping intervals and return the merged list sorted by start.
constraints: Touching intervals like [1,2] and [2,3] should merge.
hints: Sort the intervals by start time so overlaps are next to each other.
Keep a merged list and compare each new interval with the last interval in that list.
If the new start is before or equal to the last end, extend the last end; otherwise add a new interval.

questions.title: Maximum Window Sum
questions.prompt: Return the largest sum of any contiguous window of size k.
hints: First add the first k numbers; that is your first window sum.
Move the window one step by subtracting the number that left and adding the number that entered.
After each move, update the best sum if the new window is larger.
